display.purpose: Hero headings, the audit verdict headline. One per view.

label.purpose: Button text, chip labels, nav items. Not for body copy.

eyebrow.purpose: Metadata and technical context only — dates, URLs, code captions. Never for section headings.

shadows.name: ambient
shadows.purpose: Default card shadow. Every .wa-card at rest.

shadows.name: lifted
shadows.purpose: The hero floating micro-card. Rarely used.

motion.name: border-shift
motion.purpose: Input focus: border transitions to coral.

breakpoints.name: tablet
breakpoints.purpose: Results grid and improvement plan layout collapse to single column.

components.name: Coral Primary Button
components.description: The dominant call to action. Used once per screen maximum.
components.css: .ds-btn-coral { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 9px; background: #e2603f; color: #fff; border: none; border-radius: 999px; padding: 14px 24px; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: -0.01em; line-height: 1; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; min-height: 44px; box-shadow: 0 8px 20px color-mix(in srgb, #e2603f 30%, transparent); transition: opacity 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-coral:hover { opacity: 0.88; } .ds-btn-coral:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #e2603f; outline-offset: 3px; } .ds-btn-coral:disabled { opacity: 0.55; cursor: default; }

components.name: Black Primary Button
components.description: Primary action on dark surfaces and as secondary-CTA adjacent to coral.
components.css: .ds-btn-black { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 9px; background: #1c1a17; color: #fff; border: none; border-radius: 999px; padding: 14px 24px; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: -0.01em; line-height: 1; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; min-height: 44px; transition: opacity 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-black:hover { opacity: 0.88; } .ds-btn-black:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1c1a17; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: De-emphasized action. Never competes with a coral or black button on the same row.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 9px; background: #fff; color: #1c1a17; border: 1px solid #ece9e3; border-radius: 999px; padding: 14px 24px; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: -0.01em; line-height: 1; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; min-height: 44px; transition: opacity 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { opacity: 0.88; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1c1a17; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Pill label for category, status, and priority. Three semantic variants plus coral and dark.
components.html: High impact Pass Medium Needs work Locked
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: .02em; padding: 6px 11px; border-radius: 999px; line-height: 1; } .ds-chip-coral { background: #fbe9e3; color: #b9461f; } .ds-chip-green { background: #e6f0e4; color: #2f5e35; } .ds-chip-amber { background: #f7ecda; color: #8a5a0e; } .ds-chip-red { background: #f7e3dd; color: #a83228; } .ds-chip-dark { background: #1c1a17; color: #fff; }

components.name: Card
components.description: The primary content surface. White island in the Concrete background.
components.html:
Page title missing

Your page has no title tag. This affects how Google and browsers display your site.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #fff; border-radius: 22px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(40,32,24,.04), 0 2px 6px rgba(40,32,24,.04); padding: 22px; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; color: #1c1a17; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: URL and form inputs. Focus shifts border to Signal Coral.
components.css: .ds-input-wrap { width: 100%; } .ds-input { width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; background: #faf8f5; border: 1px solid #ece9e3; border-radius: 11px; padding: 10px 14px; font-size: 14px; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; color: #1c1a17; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #e2603f; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #b6b2a9; }

components.name: Pass / Fail Icon
components.description: 26px semantic state icon — always paired with a text label, never used alone.
components.html: ✓ ! ✗
components.css: .ds-ico { flex: none; display: grid; place-items: center; width: 26px; height: 26px; border-radius: 50%; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 800; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-ico-pass { background: #e6f0e4; color: #2f5e35; } .ds-ico-warn { background: #f7ecda; color: #8a5a0e; } .ds-ico-fail { background: #f7e3dd; color: #a83228; }

components.name: Score Box (Dark Surface)
components.description: The audit score display box used in the dark results header band. Workshop Black background, white text, semantic progress bar.
components.html:
42/100
Performance
components.css: .ds-score-box { background: rgba(255,255,255,.06); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.1); border-radius: 16px; padding: 16px 18px; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; color: #fff; min-width: 100px; } .ds-score-number { display: flex; align-items: baseline; justify-content: space-between; font-size: 44px; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: -0.04em; line-height: 1; } .ds-score-denom { font-size: 12px; color: rgba(255,255,255,.48); font-weight: 400; } .ds-score-label { font-size: 13.5px; font-weight: 700; margin-top: 10px; } .ds-score-track { height: 5px; border-radius: 99px; background: rgba(255,255,255,.14); margin-top: 8px; } .ds-score-fill { height: 100%; border-radius: 99px; }

narrative.overview:
A mechanic doesn't show you a glossy report — they show you the part that's failing and hand you a numbered to-do list. Website Auditor is built on that same principle: the result is the product. There's no decoration between the user and the verdict, no jargon barrier, no upsell choreography. Every pixel serves the diagnosis.

The interface earns trust the way expertise earns trust — through clarity, speed, and the confident absence of fuss. The dark header band states the result before the user has scrolled a pixel. The fix list speaks to a non-technical business owner the way a good tradesperson's quote does: ranked by what matters most, written in language anyone can act on. Signal Coral is the wrench icon of the palette — it appears only where action is required.

This system deliberately rejects the three failure modes of the category: the cold SaaS dashboard; the generic AI tool aesthetic of cream backgrounds and glassmorphism; and the bouncy startup playfulness that reads as unserious to someone who needs honest advice about their livelihood.
keyCharacteristics: Neutral-gray background that makes Signal Coral the only warm element on screen
Black header band that delivers the verdict before any copy is read
Pill-shaped buttons and chips — rounded without being soft
Semantic color palette (green/amber/red) always paired with icons, never color-only
Two-font system: Hanken Grotesk for all UI; JetBrains Mono strictly for code and metadata

rules.name: The One Warm Rule
rules.body: Signal Coral is the only warm-hued element. Every other color is neutral or semantic. A second warm accent breaks the system.

rules.name: The Semantic Pairing Rule
rules.body: Pass/warn/fail states are never conveyed by color alone. Every semantic element pairs the color with an icon or text label.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Hanken Grotesk carries every role. JetBrains Mono is restricted to URLs, code blocks, dates, and the eyebrow class. No third family.

rules.name: The Weight Contrast Rule
rules.body: Display and headings: 800. Labels: 700. Body: 400. Three steps. Do not collapse the contrast.

rules.name: The Flat Panel Rule
rules.body: Panel surfaces use background-color only — no shadows. Shadows are for cards within panels, not the panels themselves.

rules.name: The No-Decorative-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadows on static non-interactive surfaces are prohibited. If it doesn't respond to hover or focus, it doesn't shadow.

dos: Do use Signal Coral only on primary actions, the high-priority chip, and score progress fills
Do pair every semantic color (green, amber, red) with an icon or text label
Do use --muted text only for annotations and timestamps — never for body copy that carries meaning
Do maintain the 800 → 700 → 400 weight structure for display → label → body
Do use text-wrap: balance on all display and headline elements
Do use JetBrains Mono strictly for URLs, code blocks, dates, and the wa-eyebrow class
donts: Don't use cream, sand, beige, or warm-tinted backgrounds for the page background
Don't use glassmorphism decoratively — only the lock overlay uses it for a specific purpose
Don't add border-left greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards or callouts
Don't use background-clip: text with a gradient — prohibited in all cases
Don't place a small mono uppercase eyebrow above every section heading — reserve it for metadata only
Don't make the design feel cold or enterprise (no dark navy, no dense data tables as internal tooling)
Don't make the design feel playful or startup-cute (no pastel gradients, no bounce animations)
Don't reuse the Score Band pattern outside the results page header
Don't hard-code hex values for colors that have CSS custom properties
